summary: Iteration 32: Code review and UI testing of Add Implant Position modal fixes. FIX 1 (Modal Header SafeArea) and FIX 2 (Drilling Protocol) implementations verified through code review. UI testing faced navigation challenges in Expo React Native Web - procedure clicks were intercepted by overlay elements.

ui_bugs.issue: Text rendering vertically in procedure cards (T e s t P a t i e n t...) - possible CSS/flexbox issue
ui_bugs.selector: Procedure card in My Cases list

findings: Line 376: SafeAreaView wraps modal with edges=['top']
Line 789: closeBtn style has padding: 8 for easier tapping
Header row properly positioned within SafeAreaView
Lines 45-96: generateDrillingProtocol function generates system-specific drilling sequence
Lines 444-474: Drilling Protocol section displays after system selection in Step 2 (mode='choose')
Lines 451-452: Shows system name, diameters, and lengths
Lines 459-470: Renders sequential numbered steps with drill name, speed (RPM), depth, and clinical notes
Protocol generates steps based on implant diameter and boneType (defaults to D2)
Line 387: paddingTop: step === 1 ? 130 : 16 - Conditional padding for Step 1 dental chart

action_items: MANUAL VERIFICATION RECOMMENDED: Unable to fully test via Playwright due to navigation issues in Expo React Native Web. Recommend manual testing to verify:
1. Open Add Implant Position modal and verify X button is accessible (not cut off by SafeArea)
2. In Step 2, select an implant system and verify Drilling Protocol section appears with sequential steps
3. Verify all 45 implant systems load in dropdown
critical_code_review_comments: Code implementation for both fixes looks correct based on static analysis
SafeAreaView wrapper with edges=['top'] should provide proper spacing for modal header
Drilling protocol renders conditionally when selectedSystem is not null in 'choose' mode
Protocol shows: system name, diameters/lengths info, and numbered drilling steps with RPM, depth, and notes
Minor: Procedure cards in My Cases show text rendering vertically - may be a flexbox/CSS issue in React Native Web

success_rate.frontend: CODE REVIEW: 100% - UI TEST: Unable to fully complete due to navigation issues

context_for_next_testing_agent: Code review confirms both fixes are implemented correctly. UI testing had navigation difficulties - overlay elements intercepting clicks and procedure card click issues. Manual verification recommended to confirm modal header SafeArea spacing and drilling protocol display after system selection.

rca_of_the_issue.testing_challenges: Expo React Native Web renders differently from native. Procedure card clicks were intercepted by overlay elements (bottom nav bar, floating Dashboard button). The procedure cards in My Cases showed vertical text rendering issue.

code_verification.fix_1: SafeAreaView with edges=['top'] wraps modal (line 376), close button has padding: 8 (line 789)
code_verification.fix_2: Drilling Protocol section (lines 444-474) renders after selectedSystem is set with data-testid='drilling-protocol'
code_verification.regression: paddingTop: 130px conditionally applied to Step 1 content (line 387)

rca_of_the_issue.recommendation: Manual device/simulator testing recommended for final verification
